text.title: Go Error Handling Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Go Error Handling Patterns application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Domain error sentinels (ErrNotFound, ErrConflict, ErrUnauthorized, ErrValidation, ErrSystem) live in a single `internal/apperror` (or `pkg/apperror`) package. Other packages reference them.
rule.rationale: Sentinels scattered across packages duplicate; the edge translator needs one canonical set to switch on.

rule.statement: Errors crossing a layer boundary are wrapped with `fmt.Errorf("<context>: %w", err)`. Plain `return err` is allowed only when the context is already attached.
rule.rationale: Wrapping preserves the chain; errors.Is/As at the edge can still match sentinels through wrapped layers.

rule.statement: Error classification uses errors.Is (for sentinel matching) or errors.As (for typed errors carrying data). Type assertions on `err.(SomeError)` are forbidden.
rule.rationale: errors.Is/As walks the wrap chain; type assertion only matches the outermost type and silently misses wrapped errors.

rule.statement: Panic is reserved for unrecoverable programmer error (invariant violation, must-have config missing at startup). It is never used to signal recoverable errors.
rule.rationale: Panic-as-control-flow makes error paths invisible to compiler analysis and surfaces unpredictably in production.

rule.statement: HTTP/gRPC translation from domain error to status code happens in exactly one place (middleware or interceptor). Handlers return Go errors; the translator owns the mapping.
rule.rationale: Per-handler translation accumulates inconsistencies; a single translator is auditable and changeable in one place.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
